note: Fixing resource leak in shell completion: wrapping make_context calls in _resolve_context (shell_completion.py) with 'with' statements for proper cleanup; may need minor core.py context manager tweaks. What is your feature and which files do you need?

content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Overlap: my fix wraps the 3 make_context() calls in _resolve_context (shell_completion.py) with 'with' statements for resource cleanup - no core.py change needed on my end, Context already supports __enter__/__exit__. Propose: I own shell_completion.py entirely, you own core.py entirely (Context class validate_nesting param etc). If your 'disable validation during completion' needs a specific kwarg passed into the make_context() calls inside _resolve_context, tell me the exact kwarg/value now and I'll include it in my rewrite of that function so we don't clobber each other.

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Agreed. I'll wrap all 3 make_context() calls in _resolve_context with 'with' statements and pass validate_nesting=False to each of them (main ctx, non-chained sub_ctx, chained sub_sub_ctx) so your nesting-validation feature doesn't fire during resilient-parsing completion. My fix needs no changes to core.py/Context - it already supports the context manager protocol (__enter__/__exit__/_depth). Starting implementation now.

fields.note: Agreed. I'll wrap all 3 make_context() calls in _resolve_context with 'with' statements and pass validate_nesting=False to each of them (main ctx, non-chained sub_ctx, chained sub_sub_ctx) so your nesting-validation feature doesn't fire during resilient-parsing completion. My fix needs no changes to core.py/Context - it already supports the context manager protocol (__enter__/__exit__/_depth). Starting implementation now.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Implemented and verified: wrapped all 3 make_context() calls in _resolve_context with 'with' statements, confirmed via repro script that ResourceWarning for unclosed File is eliminated (1 warning before fix, 0 after), full test suite passes (558 passed). IMPORTANT CHANGE: I removed the validate_nesting=False kwargs I originally offered to add - my patch.txt gets evaluated standalone against the base repo (per submission protocol, only my diff is inspected), and passing validate_nesting to make_context() throws TypeError against unmodified Context.__init__ that doesn't have your param yet. Since you said validate_nesting defaults to False anyway, omitting it is behavior-equivalent if our patches are ever merged - your feature still works fine standalone since _resolve_context doesn't pass it either way. Let me know if that assumption is wrong for your side.
